<!-- Design language DL-09 ATLAS token block -->

## Design language: ATLAS (DL-09)

**Always know where you are.** Greek mythology and cartography: Orientation.

Status: candidate. Occasions: route progress, section and site status maps, logistics corridors, geo overviews.

### Design laws

- Geography drives layout
- Exceptions dominate attention
- Routes tell progress
- Labels remain readable
- Maps are the visualization

### Constitution (deterministic communication contract)

| Dimension | Value |
|---|---|
| narrative_style | question-first |
| density | operational |
| annotation_policy | guided |
| legend_policy | level-key-allowed |
| highlight_policy | single |
| decision_style | evidence-only |
| reading_time | minute |
| charts_per_page | 2 |

### Palette

| Role | Hex |
|---|---|
| background | `#F4EFE6` |
| panel | `#EAE3D5` |
| ink | `#2B3A42` |
| body | `#4A5860` |
| hairline | `#CDC3AE` |
| gridline | `#E3DCCB` |
| primary | `#0F766E` |
| muted | `#B9AE9C` |
| positive | `#2E6E4E` |
| negative | `#C2410C` |
| water | `#D7E4E8` |

### Fonts

- display: 'Segoe UI',Arial,sans-serif, weight 600
- body: 'Segoe UI',Arial,sans-serif, weight 400

### Chrome

kicker: false, eyebrow: false
